CCP SECTION 1281.4 IS MANDATORY. THE MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION NOTICES OR TO ISSUE A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. NO SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Consolidate Related Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WALTER CLARKE, JOHN ROSENFELD, AND OXFORD INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'S Petition To Consolidate Related Arbitration Proceedings is granted without prejudice to any party's rights that the arbitrator(s) sever the two cases. Defendants have established all the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 1281.3, judicial economy supports the granting of the motion, and the defendants will not be prejudiced by the granting of the motion. This order is not intended to/does not deprive the arbitrator(s) to whom these cases are assigned from severing the two cases for some or all purposes. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2015 Continued To Apr-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KIMBERLEY V. DEEDE, ESQ. of Kirby & McGuinn APC, Tel No. 619-398-3365 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. DANIEL G. DOWD, ESQ. of Cohen Kennedy Dowd & Quigley, Tel No. 602-252-8140, and atty. RANDOLPH GAW, ESQ. of Gaw Poe LLP, Tel No. 415-766-7451 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WALTER CLARKE, JOHN ROSENFELD, AND OXFORD INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation is granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KIMBERLY DEEDE OF KIRBY & MCGUINN, APC., TEL. NO. 1-619-398-3372 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. RANDOLPH GAW OF THE GAW/POE LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-766-7451 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CLARKE OXFORD ET. AL.;

RULING - Verified Application of Daniel G. Dowd to appear as Counsel Pro Hac Vice for the Defendants is GRANTED. Defendants have not stated whether Dowd has provided informed consent to his investor clients. At this stage Plaintiff's opposition is premature as Dowd meets the criteria for pro hac vice admission under California Rules of Court, Rule 9.40(f). Plaintiffs may later choose contest Defendants' counsel through a properly noticed Motion to Disqualify. Should an unwaivable conflict of interest arise in the future, this Court has the inherent power to revoke Dowd's pro hac vice status should he engage in conduct that would be sufficient to disqualify a California attorney. (Sheller v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1697, 1716.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 = 302/eg

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Notices And Stay Action Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-12-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ELAINE L. CHAN, ESQ. of Kirby & McGuinn, A.P.C., Tel No. 619-398-3372 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. RANDOLPH GAW, ESQ. of Gaw Poe LLP, Tel No. 415-766-7451 appearing for Defendants Walter Clarke, John Rosenfeld, and Oxford Investment Partners, LLC.

RULING - Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS WALTER CLARKE, JOHN ROSENFELD, AND OXFORD INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion To Quash Deposition Notices And Stay Action Or, In The Alternative, For Protective Order, Defendants' Motion to Stay Discovery is granted until the application for an order to arbitrate is determined. CCP section 1281.4 is mandatory. The motion to quash deposition notices or to issue a protective order is denied without prejudice. No sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
